# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { Router } from "https://deno.land/x/oak@v10.4.2/mod.ts";
import { openKv } from 'https://deno.land/std@0.153.0/database/kv.ts';

// Initialize the KV store with an identifier
const kv = await openKv(); 

const router = new Router();

router.get('/search', async (ctx) => {
  const userId = ctx.request.url.searchParams.get("user_id");
  const filterParam = ctx.request.url.searchParams.get("filter");
  
  // Dynamic key construction from user input
  let kvKey: string[] = ['users', userId, filterParam];

  try {
    // Fetch data from KV store with dynamic key
    const value = await kv.get(kvKey);
    
    ctx.response.body = JSON.stringify({ result: value });
    ctx.response.status = 200;
  } catch (error) {
    ctx.response.body = JSON.stringify({ error: 'Error fetching data' });
    ctx.response.status = 500;
  }
});

// Server setup with Oak, a simple and flexible web framework for Deno
import { Application } from "https://deno.land/x/oak@v10.4.2/mod.ts";
const app = new Application();
app.use(router.routes());
app.use(router.allowedMethods());
await app.listen({ port: 8000 });
